label: Great Lakes Fishery Research Reauthorization Bills

comments.text: Yesterday, I joined Rep. Bill Huizenga (MI-04) in introducing the bipartisan Great Lakes Fishery Research Reauthorization Act, reauthorizing funding through FY 2030. This bill will protect native species, support the $5B fishing industry, and ensure the sustainability of the Great Lakes.

The Great Lakes contain 139 native fish species. Without proper funding, we can't protect them from climate change and other threats. Yesterday, I spoke about the Great Lakes Fishery Research Reauthorization Act to reauthorize the Great Lakes Fishery research program that will expire this fall.

My bill to support scientific research in the Great Lakes was just passed by the House Natural Resources Committee! This research helps us protect and preserve our Great Lakes and the creatures that inhabit them. I hope we get to vote for the bill soon!

My bill, the Great Lakes Fishery Research Reauthorization Act, just passed out of the House!🐟🎉 This legislation will keep sea lampreys (the ugly thing eating my head), from leeching onto and killing our fish in Lake Michigan.

S2878
Great Lakes Fishery Research Reauthorization Act 📜 Signed by President. #S2878 #congress119 #senate #democrat #congress

US Congress reauthorizes Great Lakes Fishery Research Program
->SeafoodSource | #GreatLake | More from Lil Dr Glen EcoChat at BigEarthData.ai

The Senate passed the Great Lakes Fishery Research Reauthorization Act on November 19. The bill seeks to reauthorize the U.S. Geological Survey’s Great Lakes Fishery Research program, until 2030. For more, see NEMWI’s Great Lakes and Water Policy Legislative Tracker here: buff.ly/TWUopGs

On April 8th, the House Subcommittee on Water, Wildlife, and Fisheries held a hearing to discuss the Great Lakes Fishery Research Authorization Act, which would reauthorize funding for the Great Lakes Fishery Program through 2030. Watch bill sponsor Rep. Mike Quigley’s testimony at:

NEW Congressional Bill S.2878, the "Great Lakes Fishery Research Reauthorization Act", amends 16 U.S.C. 941h(d) by replacing "2025" with "2030", extending the statutory authorization for funding to monitor, assess, and research the Great Lakes Basin.
